title: SSRF redirect bypass in HTMLHeaderTextSplitter.split_text_from_url
description: HTMLHeaderTextSplitter.split_text_from_url() validates the caller-supplied URL with validate_safe_url(allow_private=False) and then fetches it with requests.get(url, ...), which follows HTTP redirects by default. Because redirect targets are not revalidated, an attacker can supply a public URL that passes validate_safe_url and have their server issue a 302 redirect to an internal/localhost/cloud-metadata endpoint (e.g. AWS IMDSv1). requests follows the redirect automatically and the fetched body is parsed into Document objects returned to the caller, defeating the intended SSRF protection.
